channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Google "could face breakup" after being found guilty of having illegal ad tech monopolies
item.description: Google has been found guilty of antitrust law violation with regards to its ads market monopolization – and the consequences could be major.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Google's digital ad network declared an illegal monopoly, joining its search engine in penalty box
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google has been branded an abusive monopolist by a federal judge for the second time in less than a year, this time for illegally exploiting some of its online marketing technology to boost the profits fueling an internet empire currently worth $1.8 trillion.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Onehouse opens up the lakehouse with Open Engines
item.description: Data lake vendor Onehouse on Thursday released Open Engines, a new capability on its platform which it says provides the ability to deploy open source engines on top of open data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Federal judge rules Google has illegal ad tech monopoly
item.description: A federal judge ruled Thursday that Google illegally acquired and maintained a monopoly over advertising technology, the second time in less than a year that the tech giant has been found in violation of antitrust laws. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Restaurant defies Cuba's tech struggles with digital menus, robot waitress
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — For Sonia Pérez, stepping into Havana’s Doña Alicia restaurant felt like entering a movie.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Don’t miss this rare Apple TV+ deal – here are the 5 shows I'm streaming alongside Severance for 70% off
item.description: This rare Apple TV+ 70% discount offer is still available, and if you're tempted to subscribe here are five Apple TV+ shows I recommend you check out.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Why Elon Musk installed his top lieutenants at a federal agency you probably haven’t heard of
item.description: The GSA's acting administrator is a Silicon Valley tech executive with expertise in rolling out artificial intelligence tools and a wife who once worked for Musk at his social media company, X.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: TikTok starts testing Footnotes, a new feature that looks a lot like X’s Community Notes
item.description: TikTok is launching its own version of community notes on the platform, called “Footnotes.” The crowd-sourced approach to moderation, where users add additional context to posts, has become increasingly popular on social media networks after X (then called Twitter) first launched it in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia expects ban on chip exports to China to cost $5.5B
item.description: Nvidia now expects new US government restrictions on exports of its H20 chip to China will cost the company as much as $5.5 billion, Reuters reports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The cybersecurity law that quietly underpins U.S. digital defenses is about to expire
item.description: Nearly a decade after Congress passed the Cybersecurity Information Sharing Act of 2015, the law is facing an uncertain future.
item.dc:creator: Max Ufberg, Fast Company

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Entire Pentagon defense tech unit to leave by May
item.description: The entire staff of the Defense Digital Service (DDS), the Pentagon’s decade-old technology development office, is leaving by the start of May, with nearly all individuals resigning, a current member of the office confirmed to The Hill on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: 8 unusual Linux commands
item.description: This post examines eight somewhat unusual Linux commands that are worth knowing. But before we get into the specific commands, you can run the command below to see whether these eight commands are installed on your system.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: China pursuing 3 alleged US operatives over cyberattacks during Asian Games
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February. A notice from the Harbin police headquarters named them as Katheryn A. Wilson, Robert J.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand in historic antitrust trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took to the witness stand on the first day of a historic antitrust trial to defend his company against allegations it illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Lawmakers call on National Archives to investigate use of Signal and Gmail by top government officials
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Homeland Security Committee say the reported use of Signal and Gmail by national security officials creates major cybersecurity risks.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Keysight tools tackle data center deployment efficiency
item.description: Test and performance measurement vendor Keysight Technologies has developed Keysight Artificial Intelligence (KAI) to identify performance inhibitors affecting large GPU deployments. It emulates workload profiles, rather than using actual resources, to pinpoint performance bottlenecks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Sony hikes PlayStation 5 console prices for Europe, UK and Australia citing economic turmoil
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sony said it will raise prices starting Monday for some PlayStation 5 video game consoles in Europe, Australia and New Zealand, citing global economic turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 4-legged hydrogen-powered robot you can actually ride
item.description: Kawasaki's CORLEO is a hydrogen-powered, AI-driven rideable robot. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reports on this new way of thinking about off-road transportation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hackers find a way around built-in Windows protections
item.description: If not properly managed, Windows Defender Application Control, a security feature with Windows, could be a potential security vulnerability.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
